title: Organize game state with an ECS
description: Model entities as ids, data as components, and behavior as systems iterating over component sets, instead of deep inheritance hierarchies.
body: Use when entity behavior is combinatorial or cache performance matters. Do not build deep GameObject inheritance trees or scatter related data across pointer-chasing objects. Actions: give entities an id, store behavior-free data in components, and write systems that query and process all entities with a given component set; lay components out contiguously for cache-friendly iteration. Validation signals include adding a behavior by composing components without touching unrelated code, and improved cache locality in profiles. Risk boundary: forcing every problem into ECS (e.g. inherently hierarchical UI) adds complexity for no gain — apply ECS where iteration and composition dominate, not everywhere.
